preamble: The transformation from the voiced bilabial plosive /b/ to the close front rounded vowel [y] is an unusual but documented phonetic shift, primarily observed as a result of lenition processes. This shift involves a significant change in both manner and place of articulation, moving from a consonant to a vowel.
phoneticEffects: Lenition, Vowelization, Allophonic variation, Historical sound change

examples.note: In some dialects of Shehret, the voiced bilabial plosive /b/ undergoes allophonic lenition to the close front rounded vowel [y], creating near-minimal pairs. For example, ʕaṣbɛ́t 'solidarity' contrasts with ʕaṣyɛ́t 'hard work; graft', both from the consonantal root /ʕ-ṣ-b/.

The sound [y] also functions as a reflex of historical *b in a closed set of lexemes in Shehret. For instance, in the dialect of Eastern Dhofar Jufa and Sadḥ, ʕējyót 'she loves' contrasts with ʕajót 'she likes', both from the root /ʕ-j-b/, where the [y] in ʕējyót reflects a historical *b.

sources: A phonetically “unnatural” class in Central and Eastern Shehret (Jibbali) - White Rose Research Online
